servinfo.title: Front Brake Disc Removal and Installation

attention4.ptxt: Failure to take proper precautions during front brake caliper removal may cause damage to the front brake flexible hose and/or the brake piston.

item.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the front brake flexible hose during front brake caliper removal.
Avoid depressing the brake pedal during the brake disc removal procedure to prevent the brake piston from being forced out of the cylinder.
Check for leakage of brake fluid when removing front brake pads and repair front brake caliper if leakage is found.
Replace all brake pads at the same time even if only one pad is defective.

s1.ptxt: Hoist vehicle and remove front wheel.
Remove front brake caliper carrier bolts (1) and remove front brake caliper (2) from steering knuckle.

item.ptxt: Hang removed front brake caliper with a wire hook to prevent brake flexible hose from bending, twisting excessively or being pulled.
Do not operate brake pedal with brake caliper removed.

s1.ptxt: Pull off front brake disc by M8 bolts (1).

attention4.ptxt: If the front brake flexible hose is twisted, the hose will be damaged.
If the front brake flexible hose is twisted, reinstall front brake caliper being careful not to let front brake flexible hose get twisted.

s1.ptxt: Install front brake caliper to steering knuckle and tighten front brake caliper carrier bolts to specified torque.
Check that brake system operates properly.
